column2: Create a frame from research, notes, requests, or transcripts when the current problem, desired outcome, boundaries, and evidence need to become explicit.
Shape ambiguous product work before implementation by defining criteria and appetite, comparing solution directions, and recording the selected shape and cut line.
Reconcile a screenshot, wireframe, mockup, whiteboard, or sketch with existing planning artifacts when visual evidence may change the frame, criteria, scope, or shape.
Map a selected or existing system into places, affordances, stores, consequences, and wiring when its concrete behavior or candidate vertical slices need clarification.
Derive states and transitions from an accepted breadboard when retries, timeouts, approvals, lifecycle stages, or multiple valid actions make behavior difficult to review.
Define plain-language inputs, outputs, branches, errors, and open decisions when a selected slice crosses a meaningful data, service, or system boundary.
Turn a selected slice into fixtures, example runs, expected outputs, edge cases, and acceptance tests when it is ready for a buildable behavioral handoff.
Plan selected work as vertical task groups with dependencies, risks, sequence, and appetite-based cuts when implementation needs more than a simple slice handoff.
Create a builder-facing kickoff document from accepted planning artifacts when a team needs a durable reference organized by shaped product territory.
Prepare a compact context packet when an implementation agent needs the authoritative planning subset, execution contract, non-goals, and verification target for one slice.
Compare implementation with the accepted breadboard and slice when code or a prototype exists and the team needs synced reality, drift, design smells, and correction options.
